title: Prevalence of Alzheimer's disease and other dementias
subtitle: The estimated share of the population with Alzheimer's disease and other dementias. This is compared between the current share and the [age-standardized](#dod:age_standardized) share, which adjusts for changes in the age structure of the population, such as aging.
caption:
Prevalence of Alzheimer's disease and other dementias

The estimated share of the population with Alzheimer's disease and other dementias. This is compared between the current share and the age-standardized share, which adjusts for changes in the age structure of the population, such as aging.

This line chart tracks the global prevalence of Alzheimer's disease and other dementias from 1990 to 2023, presenting two distinct metrics on a vertical axis ranging from 0% to 0.8%. The blue line, labeled "All ages (non age-standardized)," begins at approximately 0.4% in 1990 and follows a consistent upward trajectory throughout the period. This metric accelerates after 2010, crossing the 0.6% mark around 2016 and reaching a peak near 0.75% by 2023. In contrast, the orange line, representing the "Age-standardized" share, starts at a higher initial value of roughly 0.65% in 1990. This line remains relatively flat, hovering between 0.65% and 0.67% for most of the timeline, with a slight dip observed around 2010 before rising to just under 0.7% in 2023. The two lines intersect around the year 2019, where both metrics sit at approximately 0.67%. Following this intersection, the non-age-standardized rate continues to climb sharply, creating a visible gap between the two trends. The subtitle clarifies that the age-standardized share adjusts for changes in the age structure of the population, such as aging, distinguishing it from the raw current share shown by the blue line.
